meta.name: Tilak Poudel
meta.tagline: Building scalable systems, exploring intelligent data, and sharing timeless wisdom.

quotes.text: First solve the problem, then write the code.

quotes.translation: The undisciplined mind has no wisdom, no meditation.

quotes.text: Any fool can write code a computer understands. Good programmers write code humans understand.

quotes.translation: Elevate yourself through the power of your own mind.

quotes.text: Make it work, make it right, make it fast.

quotes.translation: You have the right to perform your actions, not to claim their fruits.

quotes.text: The best code is no code at all.

quotes.translation: The faithful and earnest seeker gains knowledge.

wisdomQuotes.translation: You have the right to act, but never to claim the fruits of action.
Elevate yourself through the power of your own mind.

wisdomQuotes.sanskrit: Tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samācara.
wisdomQuotes.translation: Without attachment, constantly perform actions that are your duty.
The faithful and earnest seeker gains knowledge.
The undisciplined mind has no wisdom, no meditation, no peace.

experience.title: Software Engineer
achievements: Improved database query performance by 30% through advanced indexing and optimization
Implemented secure mTLS authentication across microservices architecture
Built Docker-based CI/CD pipeline reducing deployment downtime by 40%
Led feature flag architecture enabling safe, iterative feature releases

experience.title: Full Stack Developer
achievements: Developed full-stack web applications for R&D projects from concept to deployment
Designed and implemented RESTful APIs with robust authentication systems
Collaborated on agile research teams delivering high-quality technical solutions

experience.title: Lecturer & Trainer
achievements: Taught Algorithms, DBMS, and Programming to 300+ undergraduate students
Designed curriculum bridging theoretical CS with practical industry applications
Mentored students in project development and career preparation

projects.title: COVID Sentiment Analysis
projects.problem: Understanding public emotional response to pandemic-era social media discourse at scale.
projects.approach: VADER + BERT-based NLP pipeline on 100K+ tweets with temporal trend analysis.

projects.title: Bhagavad Gita Network Analysis
projects.problem: Mapping the philosophical relationships and wisdom structure within the Bhagavad Gita.
projects.approach: Graph-based SNA using NetworkX to model conceptual connections between verses and teachings.

projects.title: Agricultural Trend Analysis
projects.problem: Identifying crop yield patterns and climate correlations in Nepal's agricultural data.
projects.approach: Comprehensive EDA with time-series analysis, geospatial visualization, and predictive modeling.

articles.title: Advanced SQL Optimization Patterns
articles.summary: Real-world strategies for query performance: indexing, partitioning, and avoiding N+1 pitfalls at scale.

articles.title: Feature Flags Done Right
articles.summary: Architecting safe, gradual feature releases without the complexity overhead. Practical patterns for production.

articles.title: An Engineer's Path to Data Science
articles.summary: What I wish I knew when transitioning from software engineering to data science. Honest reflections.

articles.title: The Bhagavad Gita for Software Engineers
articles.summary: Ancient wisdom applied to modern software craftsmanship — focus, detachment, and excellence in every commit.

articles.title: Understanding mTLS in Microservices
articles.summary: A practical guide to mutual TLS authentication — why it matters and how to implement it without pain.

articles.title: Teaching Algorithms That Stick
articles.summary: How to teach complex CS algorithms in ways that build intuition, not just pattern memorization.

courses.name: Algorithms & Data Structures
courses.desc: Sorting, searching, graphs, dynamic programming — taught with visual intuition and industry examples.

courses.name: Database Management Systems
courses.desc: Relational theory, normalization, SQL mastery, and the art of designing schemas that scale.

courses.name: Programming Fundamentals
courses.desc: Object-oriented design, problem decomposition, clean code practices for beginners to intermediate.

Backend Engineering.desc: Laravel, Node.js, REST APIs, GraphQL, mTLS authentication, microservices architecture.

Data Science.desc: Python, Pandas, NumPy, NLP pipelines, sentiment analysis, social network analysis, EDA.

Databases.desc: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis. Query optimization delivering 30% performance improvement.

DevOps.desc: Docker, CI/CD pipelines, reduced deployment downtime by 40%, Linux server management.

Teaching.desc: Algorithms, DBMS, Programming. 300+ students mentored over 6 years at Nepathya College.

Spiritual Wisdom.desc: Volunteer Youth preacher at ISKCON Nepal. 50+ seminars, 1000+ students. Bhagavad Gita, discipline, purpose.

recommendations.name: Richard Seltrecht
recommendations.position: Lead iOS and Android Software Manager | Wenow | France
recommendations.text: I highly recommend Tilak Poudel, who worked with us from February 2022 to December 2025 on our engagement and training platform. He demonstrated strong expertise in software development, consistently delivering reliable, high-quality improvements aligned with project needs. Tilak is collaborative, receptive to feedback, and proactive in suggesting improvements. He would be a valuable asset to any team seeking a skilled and dependable full-stack developer.

recommendations.name: Shreejana Gautam
recommendations.position: Software Quality Assurance Analyst | Talent-Share | Irvine, California, United States
recommendations.text: I highly recommend Tilak he taught me important skills in software testing, especially with Selenium and other tools. He engages students well with practical examples and creates a friendly learning environment. His deep knowledge of software devlopment helped me understand difficult concepts quickly. The hands-on projects were very helpful. He allowed me to practice what I learned in real situations. Tilak was always available for help and encouraged me to explore the subject more. Because of him, I have been able to build a solid foundation in software testing during my career.

recommendations.name: Sabir Manandhar
recommendations.position: Software Engineer @ Drive.com.au | Fullstack Developer, Software Engineering Manager
recommendations.text: I had the opportunity to manage and mentor Tilak at Dzango, where he consistently showed reliability and a willingness to learn. He approached tasks with dedication and was open to feedback, steadily improving his skills. While there were areas for growth, his perseverance and adaptability allowed him to contribute meaningfully to the team. I have no doubt that with continued experience, Tilak will keep developing their expertise. I wish him all the best in their career ahead.

recommendations.name: Sanjeev Bhandari
recommendations.text: An outstanding educator who inspires students. Tilak doesn't just teach code; he teaches problem-solving and philosophical discipline, which is rare in technical higher education.
